metadata.subject: Applied Design, Skills and Technologies
metadata.description: Students explore British Columbia's tourism industry, developing expertise in customer service, travel planning, and hospitality management. This course emphasizes experiential learning and understanding tourism's economic and cultural significance to BC communities.
metadata.general_description: An applied course exploring BC's tourism industry, customer service excellence, travel planning, and hospitality operations.

big_ideas.statement: Tourism is a significant economic driver in British Columbia that creates employment and generates revenue while shaping community identity.
big_ideas.elaboration: Understanding tourism's economic impact helps communities balance development with sustainability and cultural preservation.

big_ideas.statement: Exceptional customer service and hospitality are foundational to creating memorable travel experiences and building customer loyalty.
big_ideas.elaboration: Service excellence, attentiveness, and cultural sensitivity transform ordinary interactions into experiences that influence customer satisfaction and business success.

big_ideas.statement: Effective travel planning integrates destination knowledge, logistical coordination, and personalized service to meet diverse traveller needs.
big_ideas.elaboration: Successful travel experiences require understanding destinations, managing details, and anticipating customer needs across accommodation, transportation, and activities.

big_ideas.statement: Sustainable and responsible tourism practices ensure long-term viability while respecting local cultures, environments, and communities.
big_ideas.elaboration: Tourism professionals have a responsibility to promote practices that preserve destinations and benefit local populations.

competencies.raw: Design travel itineraries and experiences that match customer preferences and destinations
i_can_statements: I can develop customized travel itineraries based on customer preferences and budget
I can design multi-day tours incorporating activities, accommodation, and dining
I can plan experiential travel experiences that highlight authentic cultural and natural attractions
I can create themed travel packages for specific market segments

competencies.raw: Develop tourism products and services that appeal to target markets
i_can_statements: I can identify tourist markets and their preferences
I can design accommodation and dining packages
I can develop adventure, cultural, and heritage tourism experiences
I can create seasonal tourism offerings aligned with BC attractions

competencies.raw: Design hospitality operations and customer service systems for tourism businesses
i_can_statements: I can develop customer service protocols and standards
I can design check-in/check-out procedures and guest services
I can plan housekeeping and facility maintenance schedules
I can create systems for handling customer complaints and feedback

competencies.raw: Evaluate tourism services and recommend enhancements for customer satisfaction
i_can_statements: I can assess customer satisfaction with tourism services
I can identify service gaps and improvement opportunities
I can analyze competitor offerings and industry best practices
I can recommend service enhancements based on customer feedback

competencies.raw: Provide exceptional customer service in hospitality and tourism contexts
i_can_statements: I can greet guests professionally and establish rapport
I can respond to customer inquiries and resolve issues effectively
I can demonstrate cultural sensitivity and adapt communication styles
I can anticipate customer needs and provide proactive service

competencies.raw: Plan and execute travel arrangements and coordinate logistics
i_can_statements: I can research and book accommodations, transportation, and attractions
I can coordinate multi-component travel arrangements
I can manage travel documentation and special requirements
I can create detailed travel confirmations and itineraries

competencies.raw: Provide knowledgeable information about BC tourism destinations and attractions
i_can_statements: I can describe BC's major tourism regions and key attractions
I can provide cultural, historical, and natural information about destinations
I can recommend attractions and activities aligned with customer interests
I can communicate information about seasonal opportunities and events

competencies.raw: Manage hospitality operations and guest experiences
i_can_statements: I can manage front desk operations and reservation systems
I can coordinate housekeeping and maintenance activities
I can process guest payments and manage accounts
I can maintain guest records and communicate with staff

competencies.raw: Use tourism and hospitality software systems for bookings and operations
i_can_statements: I can navigate reservation and booking systems
I can manage guest profiles and booking information
I can generate confirmations and itineraries from systems
I can use property management systems for hotel operations

competencies.raw: Use digital tools to research destinations and create travel content
i_can_statements: I can research destinations using online resources and mapping tools
I can access tourism websites and travel guides
I can create digital itineraries and travel documents
I can use communication tools for customer contact and updates

competencies.raw: Apply marketing and social media tools to promote tourism services
i_can_statements: I can create promotional content for tourism services
I can manage social media presence for tourism businesses
I can use online platforms to showcase destinations and experiences
I can engage with customers through digital channels

competencies.raw: Evaluate tourism technology and digital tools for business enhancement
i_can_statements: I can assess tourism technology platforms for effectiveness
I can understand mobile apps and digital travel planning tools
I can evaluate virtual tours and immersive tourism technologies
I can identify data analytics opportunities in tourism

areas.name: BC Tourism Industry and Destinations
topics: Overview of BC's tourism industry and economic significance
Major tourism regions: Pacific Coast, Rocky Mountains, Wine Country, Greater Vancouver
Key attractions: national parks, cultural sites, natural wonders, outdoor recreation
Seasonal tourism patterns and peak tourism periods
First Peoples tourism and cultural tourism experiences
Sustainable and responsible tourism in BC

areas.name: Customer Service and Hospitality
topics: Customer service principles and expectations in tourism
Communication skills for diverse guests and situations
Handling complaints and challenging customer interactions
Cultural sensitivity and inclusivity in hospitality
Hotel front desk operations and guest services
Standards and best practices in hospitality industry

areas.name: Travel Planning and Itinerary Development
topics: Travel planning process and customer needs assessment
Transportation options: air, rail, road, cruise
Accommodation selection and booking procedures
Activity and attraction selection for different interests
Budget planning and cost management in travel
Special considerations: accessibility, dietary requirements, safety

areas.name: Tourism Business Operations
topics: Reservation and booking system management
Revenue management and pricing in tourism
Property and facility management in hospitality
Staff coordination and team communication
Safety, health, and hygiene standards in tourism
Marketing and promotion of tourism services
